castCountLine: CAST COUNT: exactly 2 distinct on-screen character identity(ies) only — Character_The_Narrator, Character_The_Old_Man. Do not invent extra people, duplicate faces, or crowd extras not listed.
actionText: INT. OLD MAN'S BEDCHAMBER - DAY. also on screen: Character_The_Old_Man. THE OLD MAN murmurs a thin reply. THE NARRATOR clasps his shoulder, all warmth. OFF-CAMERA VOICEOVER Character_The_Narrator says "that every night, just at twelve, I looked in upon him while he slept.". Character_The_Narrator still wears dark woolen waistcoat, plain white shirtsleeves, tailored trousers Camera directive: Extreme close-up, 100mm macro, lingering on Narrator's eyes and gripping hand, irony peaking with final VO line. Performance: Acting intensity 8/10: Tight unnatural grin with feverish wide-eyed stare and jaw clench Optics: f/2.0 shallow depth of field Color grading: Kodak Vision3 250D 5207 film stock, soft warm golden morning light with muted dusty ochre and pale cool-gray shadows
prompt:
STYLE LOCK: Period gothic live-action, mid-19th-century interiors; candlelight and deep shadows; desaturated cool-gray palette; naturalistic skin and fabric texture; no illustration or stylized animation

CHARACTER VARIABLES (use these identities consistently; do not redesign faces or wardrobe):
- Character_The_Narrator [The Narrator]: Lean man of middle years (about 40–50), pale sallow skin, hollow cheeks, dark disordered medium-length hair, bright intense dark eyes, thin tense mouth; plain dark wool waistcoat over white shirtsleeves, dark trousers; period clothing. Visual lock: Same lean pale face, dark disordered hair, and bright intense dark eyes in every scene; always plain dark waistcoat and shirtsleeves as default; never elderly, never white-haired, never the filmed blue eye. Voice: Male, middle years; medium-high tense pitch; precise, controlled pace that sharpens into fevered urgency; intimate confessional energy, same voice on-camera and in V.O.
- Character_The_Old_Man [The Old Man]: Also present (not shot focus); keep identity consistent: Frail elderly man, thin stooped build, sparse white hair,....

CAST COUNT: exactly 2 distinct on-screen character identity(ies) only — Character_The_Narrator, Character_The_Old_Man. Do not invent extra people, duplicate faces, or crowd extras not listed.

AUDIO: REQUIRED native Grok off-camera voiceover. Character_The_Narrator narrates exactly: "that every night, just at twelve, I looked in upon him while he slept.". Start speaking immediately with "that" — do not skip, delay, or swallow the opening word. After the last word, hold a brief natural pause with a closed mouth (about half a second); do not freeze mid-syllable or trail into empty staring. Do not lip-sync on-screen cast to this VO. Secondary layer = soft room tone / Foley. VOICE LOCK Character_The_Narrator: Male, middle years; medium-high tense pitch; precise, controlled pace that sharpens into fevered urgency; intimate confessional energy, same voice on-camera and in V.O.

PREVIOUS CLIP (already provided as video input — continue from its last frame):
INT. OLD MAN'S BEDCHAMBER - DAY. also on screen: Character_The_Old_Man. THE OLD MAN murmurs a thin reply. THE NARRATOR clasps his shoulder, all warmth. OFF-CAMERA VOICEOVER Character_The_Narrator says "So you see he would have been a very profound old man, indeed, to suspect". Character_The_Narrator still wears dark woolen waistcoat, plain white shirtsleeves, tailored trousers Camera directive: Tight close-up, 85mm lens, slow push revealing strain behind Narrator's warm expression as VO darkens. Performance: Acting intensity 7/10: Knowing half-smile, narrowed calculating eyes, arched brow and cheek twitch Optics: f/1.8 shallow depth of field, creamy background bokeh Color grading: Kodak Vision3 250D 5207 film stock, soft warm golden morning light with muted dusty ochre and pale cool-gray shadows
